title: Copy Subtree To Scope Delivery
summary: Deliver a previewed, target-only mutation that copies one canonical document subtree into another scope.
content_html:
Copy Subtree To Scope Delivery

Outcome

One singular management action copies a selected canonical document and all descendants into another existing writable non-public scope as a complete independent subtree.

The original source is unchanged. The target receives new identities, target-root placement, remapped descendant parents and internal viewer links, target-default viewability, and one coordinated generated-docs/search rebuild. No overwrite, media copy, synchronization, or return path is introduced.

Delivery Boundary

In scope:

  • write-free preview and explicit confirmation;
  • deterministic descendant expansion and copy ordering;
  • new immutable identity allocation for every target document;
  • canonical source transformation, hierarchy and internal-link remapping;
  • local and external-local target source roots, with public targets excluded;
  • target-only source writes, watcher suppression, generated-docs/search rebuild, and activity reporting;
  • one manage action, target-scope picker, result navigation, and focused durable documentation.

Out of scope:

  • checkbox or arbitrary multi-root selection;
  • copying within the same scope;
  • copying into a public scope;
  • overwrite, merge, update, synchronization, or return workflows;
  • media, download, or interactive-asset byte copying;
  • permanent copy provenance in canonical front matter;
  • static HTML snapshots or Data Sharing packages.

Code Authority To Inspect

  • source loading, descendants, formatting, timestamps, identity allocation, and target-scope roots under docs-viewer/services/;
  • the existing URL-aware viewer-link rewrite used by document-identity migration;
  • management mutation planning, service dispatch, routes, coordinated write/rebuild, watcher suppression, and activity logging;
  • management action definitions, capability projection, client calls, modal/controller ownership, and target navigation;
  • focused source-model, mutation, route, service, and runtime-module tests.

These are responsibility areas, not a permanent module inventory.

Delivery Tracker

Current state: implementation complete; the feature documents remain available for explicit closeout review.

Next checkpoint: user review of the closeout list; no retirement is authorized by delivery completion.

Complete checkpoints in order. Keep command transcripts, source inventories, link-scan results, and verification logs outside this delivery; create a temporary sibling only if that detail must survive between sessions.

[x] CPY-1 — Plan One Independent Subtree

  • [x] Resolve exactly one source root and expand its complete descendant set in deterministic source/tree order.
  • [x] Reject the current scope, public targets, unknown scopes, unavailable source roots, and targets that cannot accept managed canonical writes.
  • [x] Capture one copy timestamp and allocate a collision-free immutable id and matching target filename for every copied document without writing.
  • [x] Return a bounded preview naming the source scope/root, copied document count, target scope, and target-root placement.

Checkpoint evidence: the same unchanged source and target state produces one complete, write-free plan whose ids, paths, and hierarchy are internally valid.

[x] CPY-2 — Transform Canonical Sources

  • [x] Preserve each source body and front matter except doc_id, added_date, last_updated, parent_id, and viewable.
  • [x] Write the planned identity and copy timestamp, place the copied parent at target root, remap descendant parents, and apply the target scope's ordinary create viewability default.
  • [x] Rewrite only URL-aware Docs Viewer links whose source-scope targets are inside the copied set, preserving fragments and mapping them to the target scope and new ids.
  • [x] Leave links outside the set, semantic references, media tokens and paths, downloads, interactive assets, and source files unchanged; add no copy-provenance front matter.

Checkpoint evidence: every candidate target source parses cleanly, resolves its copied hierarchy, keeps external references intact, and contains no old copied-set parent or viewer-link target.

[x] CPY-3 — Apply One Target-Only Mutation

  • [x] Require explicit confirmation and revalidate the planned source set, target scope, identities, filenames, and absence of target collisions before the first write.
  • [x] Refuse every overwrite path and fail before mutation when the preview is stale or any planned target is no longer available.
  • [x] Write the complete planned source set through the canonical atomic-write owner and add watcher suppression for only the new target paths.
  • [x] Run one coordinated target generated-docs and search rebuild, record one bounded activity event, and return the new root id, created ids, target viewer URL, and rebuild result.

Checkpoint evidence: apply creates the complete target subtree once, changes no source-scope file, performs no overwrite, and leaves the target generated tree searchable and loadable.

[x] CPY-4 — Add The Singular Management Action

  • [x] Register Copy subtree to scope… as a singular document action on the manage-only index-view toolbar using the existing action-target and control-placement owners rather than checkbox selection or a new control system.
  • [x] Present the source root and descendant count, then offer only other currently writable non-public configured scopes in the target picker, sorted by scope id with the first selected by default.
  • [x] Use preview before the confirmation boundary, project busy/error state through the existing management action owner, and prevent duplicate submissions.
  • [x] After success, switch to the target scope and open the new copied root without implying a continuing relationship to the source.

Checkpoint evidence: the index-toolbar placement resolves the active tree document as one singular source root, unavailable targets never reach apply, and the successful result opens as an ordinary independent target document.

[x] CPY-5 — Verify, Document, And Close

  • [x] Prove pure planning and transformation for leaf, multi-level, duplicate-title, repeated-copy, local-target, and external-local-target cases, plus explicit public-target rejection.
  • [x] Prove new identity/timestamp agreement, complete hierarchy remapping, internal-link remapping, unchanged external/media references, target-default viewability, and a byte-unchanged source scope.
  • [x] Prove stale preview, target collision, invalid target, rebuild failure reporting, route/service response, and action-definition boundaries at the lowest stable layer.
  • [x] Update Create And Import Endpoints, run git diff --check and the focused private-path/secret scan, then present the explicit feature closeout list.

Checkpoint evidence: all Done When conditions pass, the durable endpoint owner explains the shipped workflow, and disposable implementation evidence remains outside this tracker.

Done When

  • one selected document and all descendants can be copied to any other currently writable non-public configured scope;
  • every copied document has a new immutable id, matching filename, copy-time added_date/last_updated, and target-default viewability;
  • the copied parent is at target root and every descendant resolves only through new target ids;
  • copied-set viewer links target the new subtree while external links, semantic references, and asset references retain their original meaning;
  • preview writes nothing, apply overwrites nothing, repeated copies remain additive, and the source scope is unchanged;
  • target source and generated documents are complete, searchable, and loadable after one coordinated rebuild;
  • the management action uses the existing singular target, capability, modal, busy-state, and navigation owners;
  • focused planner, mutation, route/service, generated-result, and runtime integration evidence passes;
  • durable endpoint documentation describes the current copy contract and the feature documents are ready for explicit closeout review.
